repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Described in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/discussions/38073.

### Problem
vim.fs.joinpath concatenates empty path segments:

```lua
print(vim.fs.joinpath('', 'after/lsp', '')) -- '/after/lsp/'
print(vim.fs.joinpath('', '')) -- '/'
```

Especially problematic if the empty segment is the first segment, as that converts the path to an absolute path.

### Solution
Ignore empty (length of 0) path segments.

Note:
I tested multiple versions of removing the empty parts and this was by far the most performant one. To give an idea:

Test function:

```lua
local function test(func)
  local t = vim.uv.hrtime()
  for _ = 1, 100000, 1 do
    func('', 'this/is', 'a/very/long/path', '', 'it', 'really', 'is')
  end
  print(math.floor((vim.uv.hrtime() - t) / 1e6), 'ms')
end
```

- with Iter():filter() --> 370 ms
- with table.remove() --> 168 ms
- building new segments table --> 208 ms
- with vim.tbl_filter --> 232 ms
- Instead of gsub split on `/` in all parts --> 1870 ms

comment_data.body:
It seems that there is still a problem with this implementation if there is 2 or more adjacent empty segments.

```lua
print(joinpath('', '', 'foo', 'bar', '', '')) -- '/foo/bar/'
print(joinpath('', '', '', '')) -- '/'
```

Also, removing elements while iterating looks like a code smell. [`table.remove()`](https://neovim.io/doc/user/luaref/#table.remove()) mentions that it shifts the elements, so the `for` loop is probably not seeing the second `''` since it ended in the position of the previous one.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -116,17 +116,24 @@ end
 
 --- Concatenates partial paths (one absolute or relative path followed by zero or more relative
 --- paths). Slashes are normalized: redundant slashes are removed, and (on Windows) backslashes are
---- replaced with forward-slashes. Paths are not expanded/resolved.
+--- replaced with forward-slashes. Empty segments are removed. Paths are not expanded/resolved.
 ---
 --- Examples:
 --- - "foo/", "/bar" => "foo/bar"
+--- - "", "after/plugin" => "after/plugin"
 --- - Windows: "a\foo\", "\bar" => "a/foo/bar"
 ---
 ---@since 12
 ---@param ... string
 ---@return string
 function M.joinpath(...)
-  local path = table.concat({ ... }, '/')
+  local segments = { ... }
+  for i, segment in ipairs(segments) do
+    if #segment == 0 then
+      table.remove(segments, i)
+    end

comment_data.body: Thanks for the proper testing! It indeed felt a bit iffy to remove elements in the loop, but my initials tests worked and this solution was fast. I'll switch to another approach and add your test cases.

comment_data.body: if we are only collecting stuff into `segments` to join into a string, maybe using a stringbuffer is more performant: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/583308f5991c4c55d3f04e456254d95433033c72/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/rpc.lua#L4
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -116,21 +116,30 @@ end
 
 --- Concatenates partial paths (one absolute or relative path followed by zero or more relative
 --- paths). Slashes are normalized: redundant slashes are removed, and (on Windows) backslashes are
---- replaced with forward-slashes. Paths are not expanded/resolved.
+--- replaced with forward-slashes. Empty segments are removed. Paths are not expanded/resolved.
 ---
 --- Examples:
 --- - "foo/", "/bar" => "foo/bar"
+--- - "", "after/plugin" => "after/plugin"
 --- - Windows: "a\foo\", "\bar" => "a/foo/bar"
 ---
 ---@since 12
 ---@param ... string
 ---@return string
 function M.joinpath(...)
-  local path = table.concat({ ... }, '/')
-  if iswin then
-    path = path:gsub('\\', '/')
+  local n = select('#', ...)
+  ---@type string[]
+  local segments = {}
+  for i = 1, n do
+    local s = select(i, ...)
+    if s and #s > 0 then
+      segments[#segments+1] = s
+    end
   end
-  return (path:gsub('//+', '/'))
+
+  local path = table.concat(segments, '/')

@@ -116,21 +116,30 @@ end
 
 --- Concatenates partial paths (one absolute or relative path followed by zero or more relative
 --- paths). Slashes are normalized: redundant slashes are removed, and (on Windows) backslashes are
---- replaced with forward-slashes. Paths are not expanded/resolved.
+--- replaced with forward-slashes. Empty segments are removed. Paths are not expanded/resolved.
 ---

+--- - "", "after/plugin" => "after/plugin"
 --- - Windows: "a\foo\", "\bar" => "a/foo/bar"
 ---
 ---@since 12
 ---@param ... string
 ---@return string
 function M.joinpath(...)
-  local path = table.concat({ ... }, '/')
-  if iswin then
-    path = path:gsub('\\', '/')
+  local n = select('#', ...)
+  ---@type string[]
+  local segments = {}
+  for i = 1, n do
+    local s = select(i, ...)
+    if s and #s > 0 then
+      segments[#segments+1] = s
+    end
   end
-  return (path:gsub('//+', '/'))
+
+  local path = table.concat(segments, '/')

comment_data.body: Seems to be just more code around the same idea: append to table and concat afterwards.

comment_data.body: That is true for the "shim", but the luajit `string.buffer` presumably is optimized somehow.

repo.name: neovim

title: fix(vim.fs): joinpath should ignore empty segments
